questions.questionText: Which vaccine is contraindicated in pregnancy as a live vaccine?
questions.explanation: MMR is a live attenuated vaccine and is contraindicated during pregnancy. Tetanus toxoid, inactivated influenza, and hepatitis B vaccines are non-live and may be used when indicated. This is a standard PSM-immunization distinction.

questions.questionText: The cold chain equipment used to keep vaccines cold during outreach sessions is:
questions.explanation: A vaccine carrier is used for transporting vaccines during outreach sessions while maintaining cold chain. Deep freezers and ice-lined refrigerators are fixed storage equipment. Walk-in coolers are higher-capacity central storage facilities.

questions.questionText: Pulse Polio programme is an example of:
questions.explanation: Pulse Polio consists of supplementary immunization activities delivered as mass campaigns to rapidly increase population immunity and interrupt transmission. It is not primordial prevention, chemoprophylaxis, or secondary prevention. This is a common public health programme question.
